channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: EverQuest II Announces Launch Details for Next TLE, Wuoshi
item.content:encoded:
Details for EverQuest II’s next Time Locked Expansion server, Wuoshi, are here, ahead of its opening in June. 

The server opens on June 9th at noon PT to All Access members. Wuoshi was mentioned in last month’s Producer’s Letter along with a few details, but along with an overview, there’s a FAQ addressing questions that came up since that letter was shared. Also in that April Producer’s Letter was a cryptic first hint at the next expansion, but that announcement is still some time away. 

Wuoshi will be a standard TLE with free trade and without PvP, starting with all expansions up to Echoes of Faydwer unlocked. Expansions will unlock every three months, ending with Chaos Descending. The expanded start means that classes available through Echoes of Faydwer will be available from Wuoshi’s opening, with others unlocking with their expansions.

The expanded start also means that nearly all live events in 2026 after the server launch can happen without modifications, except Tinkerfest. Another modification means that Hunters rewards are going away, because the content was too easy and feedback indicated that those rewards were contributing to “trivialization of content”. 

You can read the announcement and FAQ for more on what to expect and what is or isn’t changing for Wuoshi.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/everquest-ii-announces-launch-details-for-next-tle-wuoshi-2000138022
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sky: Children of the Light's Pirate-Themed Event Returns This Year For Treasure Hunt Fun
item.content:encoded:
The multiplatform social adventure MMO Sky: Children of the Light has announced the latest limited-time event, the returning “Days of Treasure,” sending players off on little treasure hunts.  

In this pirate-themed event, players can team up with a team of spirits to search for hidden treasure using a map and earn points for one of two teams, The Scavengers versus The Realm spirits. These quests can be done once a day, and they take place throughout Sky’s many maps. 

Doing the quests will earn players Event Tickets, which can be earned for some nifty pirate-themed rewards, including a cool outfit; as usual, these Event Tickets can also be found around the game for free. 

This limited-time Sky event also runs parallel to the longer “Season of the Carnival,” an overarching series of quests and items with carnival-themed games and missions, even allowing players to make their own game using the Memories features. Plus, Sky is always building out its capabilities, with the “Presence” feature in early stages of launch. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/sky-children-of-the-lights-pirate-themed-event-returns-this-year-for-treasure-hunt-fun-2000138020
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
